methods.description: Process for selecting sampling stations and collecting water, periphyton, and sediment samples for analysis during different seasonal periods.

steps.description: The selection of sampling stations was based on the size of reservoirs, inflows of tributaries, and the depth of the system.
steps.searchable_summary: select sampling stations based on reservoir size, tributary inflows, and system depth

steps.description: Samples of water and periphyton were collected during the dry (August 2013) and the rainy periods (January 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: collect water and periphyton samples during dry and rainy periods

steps.description: Surface sediments were collected only during the dry period.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface sediment samples during dry period

steps.description: Water samples along a vertical profile were collected using a van Dorn bottle.
steps.searchable_summary: collect vertical profile water samples with van Dorn bottle

steps.description: Temperature, pH, and electric conductivity were measured in situ with a Horiba U50 multiparameter portable probe.
steps.searchable_summary: measure temperature, pH, and conductivity in situ with Horiba U50 probe

steps.description: Samples of water were collected for the analysis of total nitrogen, total phosphorus, and soluble reactive silicon.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water samples for nitrogen, phosphorus, and silicon analysis

steps.description: For the determination of chlorophyll a, a water sub-sample was filtered through a glass-fiber filter, followed by a 24 h extraction with 90% ethanol.
steps.searchable_summary: filter water sub-sample and extract chlorophyll a with ethanol

steps.description: Periphyton samples were obtained by scraping submersed parts of the aquatic macrophyte Eichhornia crassipes.
Plankton and periphyton samples were fixed with an acetic Lugol solution.
steps.searchable_summary: fix plankton and periphyton samples with acetic Lugol solution

steps.description: Surface sediments were collected with a UWITEC gravity corer, and the top 2 cm sections of the core were used for analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface sediments with UWITEC gravity corer and section top 2 cm

methods.description: Procedures for analyzing chemical and biological properties of collected samples, including nutrient analysis, chlorophyll determination, and diatom identification.

steps.description: The Trophic State Index (TSI) was calculated using the data of total phosphorus and chlorophyll a.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Trophic State Index using phosphorus and chlorophyll a data

steps.description: The oxidation of the diatom organic matter followed the process described in Battarbee (1986).
steps.searchable_summary: oxidize diatom organic matter following Battarbee (1986)

steps.description: Permanent slides were prepared with Naphrax as mounting medium.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare permanent slides with Naphrax mounting medium

steps.description: Diatoms were identified from a population sample using a Zeiss Axioskop 2 plus equipped with phase-contrast and an image capture system, at a magnification of 1000×.
steps.searchable_summary: identify diatoms using Zeiss Axioskop 2 plus at 1000× magnification

steps.description: Counting was conducted according to Battarbee (1986) with criteria for quantification limit.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct diatom counting with Battarbee criteria

steps.description: Species richness was defined as the number of species per sample, and the Simpson's dominance index was calculated using density data.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate species richness and Simpson's dominance index

steps.description: Canonical Correspondence Analysis (CCA) was performed to evaluate the relationship between biological and environmental data matrices.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CCA to evaluate biological-environmental relationships

steps.description: In the CCA, the Monte Carlo permutation procedure (999 permutations) was used to test for significance between species and environmental variables.
steps.searchable_summary: use Monte Carlo permutation to test CCA significance
